raw_cv_text:
[CANDIDATE_NAME] 
[PHONE] | [EMAIL] | Toronto, ON, Canada 

PROFESSIONAL STATEMENT 

Formally trained engineer with extensive experience in designing and implementing high-throughput screening 
assays and laboratory automation systems using confocal microscopes, image analysis tools, and scripts (Python, 
ImageJ, MATLAB).  Proven ability to independently manage complex projects in various fields from concept to 
validation, with strong proficiency in cell assays, microscopy, and material characterization. Motivated self-starter 
evidenced by enrollment into data science courses and creation of tools/scripts to streamline processes or tasks. 

EDUCATION 

Ph.D Chemical Engineering Candidate, Shoichet Lab, University of Toronto 
GPA:4.0/4.0 
Relevant Coursework: 
UofT DSI Data Science Certificate –Database structure and querying using SQLite, version tracking using [CANDIDATE_NAME] 
CHE1148H Process Data Analytics – Python and [CANDIDATE_NAME] notebooks, Data handling and analysis using Pandas, 

       Sept 2020 – Present 

machine learning algorithms 

B.ASc Nanotechnology Engineering, University of Waterloo  
GPA:3.8/4.0 

  Sept 2013 – May 2018 

WORK EXPERIENCE 

Graduate Researcher 
The Shoichet Lab (PI: Dr. [CANDIDATE_NAME])  

July 2020 – Present 

•  Develop and modify polymeric systems for the investigation of liver and lung disease models using disease 

and healthy patient cells. 

•  Create automated imaging and analysis processes for high throughput imaging systems (Opera Phenix) and 

confocal microscopy systems for the laboratory.  

•  Utilize Excel, Python, MATLAB, and ImageJ to clean, analyze, and visualize large amounts of quantitative and 

qualitative data. Automated processes for time savings of 10 fold at minimum. 

•  Conduct literature review and keep up to date with literature to identify gaps and trends in fields. 

Fellow 
Biomineralization and Biomimetics Lab (PI: Dr. [CANDIDATE_NAME])  
Harvard University & Massachusetts Eye and Ear 

Oct 2018 – Oct 2019 

•  Developed  microbial  and  cellular  adhesion  assays  on  omniphobic  materials  for  tympanostomy  tubes 

• 

showing a reduction of up to 99% of bacterial attachment. 
Interfaced  with  the  animal  study  and  development  team  to  provide  analysis,  feedback,  and  potential   
improvements for in vivo studies. 

Hardware Engineering Intern 
Verily (formerly Google Life Sciences) 

May 2017 – Aug 2017 

•  Spearheaded  the  formulation  and  characterization  of  a  novel  polymer  platform  for  implantable  devices 

resulting in 7 different formulations. 
Initiated biocompatibility tests in compliance with ISO 10993. 
Interfaced with various teams to develop new in-house procedures and testing protocols.  

• 
• 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Research Assistant 
Biomechanics and Cell Mechanics Laboratory (PI: Dr. [CANDIDATE_NAME]) 
Soochow University 

Sep 2015 – Apr 2016 

•  Developed, synthesized, and conducted experiments on novel polymers for tissue engineering applications. 
•  Designed, prototyped, and tested multiple versions of knee braces for genu [CANDIDATE_NAME] (knock- knee) treatment 

using CAD and 3D printing.  

•  Wrote technical documents on completed work resulting in three published papers. 

Research Assistant 
Micro Engineering Dynamics and Automation Lab (PI: Dr. [CANDIDATE_NAME]) 
University of Calgary 

Jan 2015 – Apr 2015 

•  Revised and extensively tested a novel aluminum-air battery. Led to a 24-fold increase in performance. 
•  Assembled enclosures and holders for battery testing using CAD, 3D printing, and machining. 
•  Designed and built a small-scale roller system and environmental controllers using Arduino.  
•  Completed work set the foundation for one publication. 

INVOLVEMENT 

Co-chair and Advisor 
Toronto Biomedical Engineering Conference 

September 2022-Present 

•  Oversee conference planning duties such as inviting speakers, developing workshops, managing a team 
of 11 people, as well as booking venues and catering for the event. Increased attendance by 66.8% from 
2022-2023 

Business Lead and Technical Advisor 
University of Waterloo Nanorobotics Group (UW_NRG) 

2013 – 2018 

•  Designed, tested, and debugged multiple microrobot setups as well as their control systems. 
•  Constructed stages for the microrobots using machining and 3D printing. 
•  Wrote and presented proposals to potential sponsors. Secured over $20,000 in funds for UW_NRG. 
•  Competed in the annual Mobile Microrobotics Challenge at the International Conference on Robotics and 
Automation. Two 1st place finishes (2013, 2016), one 2nd place finish (2015), one 3rd place finish (2014). 

SIDEPROJECT 

Japan Top Restaurants Map and Spreadsheet – Utilized Python to scrape over 5000 restaurant entries from a 
Japanese food review website for the top restaurants in multiple cities. Organized, filtered, and displayed the 
restaurants on Google Sheets for offline access/planning as well as Google maps for quick navigation. The tool was 
released publicly on Reddit and garnered over 48,000 views and 1,700 shares within a day. 
https://github.com/cwfling/Top-Japanese-Restaurants-Tabelog 

SKILLS 

Programming: Python, MATLAB, ImageJ, SQL , Java 
Software: Microsoft Office, GraphPad, Google Suite (Docs, Sheets, Slides), Adobe Photoshop/Illustrator, 
Solidworks 
Languages: English, Chinese (Hakka dialect)

pitch_text:
[EXCEL SOURCE]: Engineer, Analyst/Data Scientist, Project manager, IP  


[FILE: clarify_module-Christopher.pdf]
Question #1: My Ikigai 

My Ikigai centers on accelerating innovation through automation and data intelligence. 

The intersection of my passions and skills lies in identifying bottlenecks in complex scientific 
processes and removing them. I love the "detective work" of analyzing a problem (whether in 
batteries, tissue engineering, or metallurgy) and the satisfaction of building a tool that solves it 
permanently. My talents in Python, microscopy, and hardware engineering allow me to bridge 
the gap between traditional wet-lab science and modern data science—a gap that the world 
(specifically the biotech and medical technology sectors) desperately needs to close to improve 
healthcare outcomes. 

I am driven to create a lasting impact by building systems that allow scientists to focus on 
discovery rather than repetitive tasks. To align my career with this, I am pivoting from pure 
academic research to industry roles where scalability is key, such as Data Science or 
Automation Engineering. I am ready to compromise on the "freedom" of academic exploration to 
achieve the tangible, high-velocity impact of industry product development. Living my Ikigai 
means constantly learning new tools (like my recent deep dive into SQL and Machine Learning) 
to ensure I am always the most efficient problem-solver in the room. 

Question #2: My Ideal Day 

My ideal professional day is a mix of technical problem-solving and cross-functional 
collaboration. It starts with a brief team stand-up where we align on the day's sprint—I thrive in 
environments where engineering and science teams interface, like I did at Verily. 

The morning is deep-work time: I am coding a Python script to automate a data pipeline or 
refining an image analysis algorithm. I enjoy the "flow state" of debugging and optimizing code 
to shave seconds or minutes off a process. [CANDIDATE_NAME] is social; I enjoy connecting with colleagues 
from different backgrounds to learn about their specific challenges. 

In the afternoon, I switch to "bridge" mode—translating complex data insights into actionable 
summaries for stakeholders, similar to how I managed the Biomedical Engineering Conference. 
I might spend an hour testing a new hardware prototype or validating a model against real-world 
data. The day ends with a sense of tangible progress—having built a tool or generated an 
insight that didn't exist in the morning. 

Question #3: Job Titles 

1.  Data Scientist (Biotech/Pharma): This role is my primary target. It leverages my UofT 
Data Science certification and my PhD experience in handling large datasets. It allows 
me to use my domain knowledge in biology/chemistry to interpret data more effectively 
than a generalist data scientist could. 

2.  Automation Process Engineer: I have a proven track record of reducing manual labor 

(e.g., 10-fold time savings in the Shoichet Lab). I enjoy the hardware-software 

 intersection of this role, designing high-throughput screening assays that function 
autonomously. 

3.  R&D Engineer (Medical Devices): Drawing on my experience at Verily and my 

background in materials, this role appeals to my desire to build tangible products. It 
combines my skills in ISO compliance, prototyping, and experimental design. 

4.  Product Manager (Technical): With my experience leading the Nanorobotics Group 

and organizing conferences, I have the leadership and organizational skills to manage 
product lifecycles. I enjoy the strategic aspect of aligning technical capabilities with 
market needs. 

Question #4: S.M.A.R.T Goals 

●  Goal 1 (Career): Secure a full-time position as a Data Scientist or Automation Engineer 

in the Greater Toronto Area or remotely by May 2026. 

○  Measurable: Apply to 5 relevant jobs per week and secure at least 3 interviews 

per month. 

○  Attainable: leveraging my PhD defense timeline and current interview 

momentum. 

●  Goal 2 (Technical Skill): Complete the UofT DSI Data Science Certificate and build one 

end-to-end MLOps project (deploying a model to the cloud) by April 1, 2026. 

○  Measurable: Certificate issued; GitHub repository live with deployment 

documentation. 

○  Realistic: I am already enrolled and have a foundation in Python. 
●  Goal 3 (Networking): Expand my professional network outside of academia by 

connecting with 20 industry professionals on LinkedIn in the HealthTech space by June 
2026. 

○  Measurable: Send 5 personalized connection requests weekly; schedule 1 coffee 

chat per month. 

○  Time-bound: Start immediately, review progress monthly. 

 
 

[FILE: pitch_module-Christopher.pdf]
Selected Job Offer Position: Data Scientist, Drug Discovery (Hypothetical Biotech Company) 
Key Requirements: 

●  PhD in Engineering, Biology, or related field. 
●  Proficiency in [CANDIDATE_NAME] (Pandas, Scikit-learn) and SQL. 
●  Experience with high-throughput screening data and image analysis. 
●  Ability to translate biological questions into data problems. 

The Elevator Pitch 

"Hi, I’m Christopher. I’m a PhD candidate in Chemical Engineering at U of T, but I think of myself 
as an engineer who speaks fluent 'data.' 

In my research, I realized that the biggest bottleneck wasn't the science itself, but how we 
handled the data. I didn't just run experiments; I built automated Python pipelines and imaging 
systems that reduced our analysis time by 10-fold. I essentially turned our wet lab into a data 
factory. 

I noticed you’re looking for someone to bridge the gap between high-throughput screening and 
data science. Because I’ve actually done the pipetting and the coding—from analyzing confocal 
microscopy images to scraping 5,000 datasets for my own side projects—I can clean and 
interpret your biological data faster and with more context than a standard data scientist. 

I’m looking to bring that efficiency to a team like yours where I can help accelerate the discovery 
pipeline. I’d love to hear how your team is currently handling its screening data." 

○

hard_skills_analysis.skill_name: Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie
Expérience dans les domaines médical et/ou pharmaceutique et/ou de la cytométrie en flux
Connaissance des procédures opérationnelles standard (SOP) générales

culture_analysis.dissonance_analysis: Les valeurs de l'entreprise telles que l'innovation, l'excellence opérationnelle et le développement du personnel sont en accord avec les motivations et les passions du candidat. Cependant, il existe un risque de friction lié à la recherche de liberté créative et de flexibilité par le candidat, qui pourrait être contraint par les exigences de conformité et de qualité de l'entreprise.
culture_analysis.retention_risk: Risque moyen : Le candidat semble avoir une bonne adéquation avec les valeurs et les objectifs de l'entreprise, mais il pourrait être tenté de quitter si ses besoins de liberté créative et de développement personnel ne sont pas satisfaits.

flags: ⛔ [CRITICAL] LOCALISATION : Candidat non mobile pour poste sur site (Toronto, Ontario).

rhetoric_analysis.analysis_text: La structure du pitch est claire et bien organisée, avec une introduction qui présente l'Ikigai du candidat, suivie de sections qui détaillent son idéal professionnel et ses objectifs de carrière. Le vocabulaire est précis et technique, démontrant une bonne maîtrise du domaine. Cependant, certaines phrases sont un peu longues et pourraient être condensées pour améliorer la clarté. Le candidat utilise efficacement la logique 'Action -> Résultat' pour démontrer son impact et sa confiance, ce qui renforce la persuasion.

readiness_diagnostic.cot_reasoning: Le candidat présente des forces en termes de communication et de valeurs alignées avec l'entreprise, mais il manque de compétences techniques essentielles et présente des risques de rétention et de logistique. Les compétences techniques manquantes incluent le diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie, l'expérience dans les domaines médical et/ou pharmaceutique et/ou de la cytométrie en flux, la connaissance des procédures opérationnelles standard (SOP) générales, la connaissance des réglementations GLP, MS-Office, Outlook, Anglais et français (niveau C1). Les risques de rétention sont liés à la recherche de liberté créative et de développement personnel, qui pourrait être contrainte par les exigences de conformité et de qualité de l'entreprise. Les risques logistiques incluent l'absence d'information sur le visa et la non-mobilité du candidat pour le poste sur site à Toronto, Ontario.
hard_skills_gap: Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie
Expérience dans les domaines médical et/ou pharmaceutique et/ou de la cytométrie en flux
Connaissance des procédures opérationnelles standard (SOP) générales
readiness_diagnostic.risk_assessment: Les risques de rétention et de logistique sont présents, mais ils ne sont pas insurmontables. Le candidat pourrait être formé pour acquérir les compétences techniques manquantes, et les risques de rétention pourraient être atténués en offrant des opportunités de développement personnel et de liberté créative. Cependant, les risques logistiques liés au visa et à la mobilité du candidat nécessitent une attention particulière.

interview_guide.question: Nous avons noté des contraintes (Visa/Lieu). Quelle est votre flexibilité réelle à ce sujet ?
Il nous manque des preuves sur : Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie. Pouvez-vous nous donner un exemple concret ?
interview_guide.rationale: Vérifier si c'est un oubli du CV.

audit_trail: TwinBuilder V3.1: Ingestion Smart terminée. ID: 95293f0c-0456-4abb-a555-7a93b7b775a8
